text:
To determine how many times Sandra needs to babysit, we first need to calculate the total number of eggs required to make 15 Spanish flans. Each Spanish flan requires 3 eggs.

First, calculate the total number of eggs needed:
\[ 15 \text{ flans} \times 3 \text{ eggs per flan} = 45 \text{ eggs} \]

Next, we need to find out how many times Sandra needs to babysit to collect 45 eggs. Since Sandra's neighbor gives her 9 eggs each time she babysits, we divide the total number of eggs needed by the number of eggs she receives per babysitting:

\[ \frac{45 \text{ eggs}}{9 \text{ eggs per babysitting}} = 5 \]

Therefore, Sandra needs to babysit 5 times to have enough eggs to make 15 Spanish flans.
